title: lichen_l2.c:1867-1868 peer_try_all_pubkeys modifies rx_ctx even on success
description:
In lichen_l2_input() at lines 1845-1868:
```c
struct lichen_link_rx_ctx rx_ctx = {
    .peer_pubkey = NULL,
    .peer_eui64 = NULL,
    ...
};
uint8_t src_eui64[8] = {0};

ret = peer_try_all_pubkeys(&rx_ctx, &replay_table, data, len,
                           rx_ipv6_buf, &ipv6_len, src_eui64);
```

peer_try_all_pubkeys() modifies rx_ctx.peer_pubkey and rx_ctx.peer_eui64 on success (lines 593-595 in peer_try_all_pubkeys). These are set to point into peer_table entries.

After peer_try_all_pubkeys() returns, rx_ctx.peer_pubkey and rx_ctx.peer_eui64 point into peer_table. But rx_ctx is a local variable that goes out of scope at the end of lichen_l2_input(). The pointers are never dereferenced after peer_try_all_pubkeys() returns.

Actually, looking at the code after line 1868:
- Line 1870-1874: error handling returns early
- Line 1877-1883: validate ipv6_len, may return early
- Line 1896-1897: LOG_DBG uses src_eui64, not rx_ctx
- Line 1900: secure_zero(rx_link_key)

rx_ctx is never accessed after line 1868. The dangling pointers are harmless because rx_ctx goes out of scope.

NO BUG - rx_ctx is not used after the call.
